channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: Ruling overturns Senegal's Africa Cup title and declares Morocco the champion
item.description: Morocco was stunningly awarded the Africa Cup of Nations title on Tuesday by governing body judges who overturned Senegal's victory in a chaotic final in January.
item.content:encoded:
Morocco was stunningly awarded the Africa Cup of Nations title on Tuesday by governing body judges who overturned Senegal's victory in a chaotic final in January.

(Image credit: Youssef Loulidi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Venezuela beats U.S. 3-2, wins first World Baseball Classic title
item.description: Venezuela won the World Baseball Classic for the first time, rebounding from a blown eighth-inning lead to beat the United States 3-2 Tuesday night on Eugenio Suárez's tiebreaking double in the ninth
item.content:encoded:
Venezuela won the World Baseball Classic for the first time, rebounding from a blown eighth-inning lead to beat the United States 3-2 Tuesday night on Eugenio Suárez's tiebreaking double in the ninth

(Image credit: Lynne Sladky)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: EU offers to pay Ukraine to fix oil pipeline at the center of Ukraine-Hungary feud
item.description: EU officials say they have offered money and technical help to Ukraine to fix a key oil pipeline to Central Europe. They hope that will persuade Hungary to drop its veto on major aid to Ukraine.
item.content:encoded:
EU officials say they have offered money and technical help to Ukraine to fix a key oil pipeline to Central Europe. They hope that will persuade Hungary to drop its veto on major aid to Ukraine.

(Image credit: Sven Kaestner)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Daniel Biss wins Democratic primary for closely-watched Illinois House seat
item.description: Biss, the mayor of Evanston, Ill., topped political newcomer Kat Abughazaleh, a first-time candidate who ran as an unapologetic progressive in the race to succeed longtime incumbent Jan Schakowsky.
item.content:encoded:
Biss, the mayor of Evanston, Ill., topped political newcomer Kat Abughazaleh, a first-time candidate who ran as an unapologetic progressive in the race to succeed longtime incumbent Jan Schakowsky.

(Image credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: Arizona AG files criminal charges against Kalshi over 'illegal gambling'
item.description: Arizona is the first state to allege the prediction market company has committed criminal violations, accusing it of running an unlicensed gambling operation.
item.content:encoded:
Arizona is the first state to allege the prediction market company has committed criminal violations, accusing it of running an unlicensed gambling operation.

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Judge orders 1,000 Voice of America staffers back to work in rebuke to Kari Lake
item.description: A federal judge has ordered more than a thousand Voice of America staffers back to work by Monday. It's a major defeat for the Trump administration's effort to cut the news outlet to the bones.
item.content:encoded:
A federal judge has ordered more than a thousand Voice of America staffers back to work by Monday. It's a major defeat for the Trump administration's effort to cut the news outlet to the bones.

(Image credit: SAUL LOEB/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Iran expert says Trump's 'war of choice' has morphed into a 'war of necessity'
item.description: With the Strait of Hormuz blocked, policy expert Karim Sadjadpour says the war in Iran is becoming increasingly complicated: "I don't think President Trump ... understood what he was getting into."
item.content:encoded:
With the Strait of Hormuz blocked, policy expert Karim Sadjadpour says the war in Iran is becoming increasingly complicated: "I don't think President Trump ... understood what he was getting into."

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: USS Ford has seen war, fire and plumbing woes as it nears a record long deployment
item.description: The Ford's crew left Norfolk, Va., on June 24, initially bound for the Mediterranean. More than nine months later, the crew is now in the Red Sea for the war with Iran with no clear return date.
item.content:encoded:
The Ford's crew left Norfolk, Va., on June 24, initially bound for the Mediterranean. More than nine months later, the crew is now in the Red Sea for the war with Iran with no clear return date.

(Image credit: Jonathan Klein)
item.dc:creator: Steve Walsh

item.title: Khamenei's killing renews questions about U.S. assassinating foreign leaders
item.description: Technology allowed the U.S. and Israel to kill Iran's Supreme Leader, but raised longstanding questions about whether the U.S. as a democracy should be assassinating foreign leaders.
item.content:encoded:
Technology allowed the U.S. and Israel to kill Iran's Supreme Leader, but raised longstanding questions about whether the U.S. as a democracy should be assassinating foreign leaders.

(Image credit: Ahmad Al-Rubaye)
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: Adam Gopnik salutes the immigrant experience that shaped American music
item.description: In a conversation with pianist Lara Downes, the New Yorker staff writer says music in America will keep evolving as long as the country keeps an open door to new people and new sounds.
item.content:encoded:
In a conversation with pianist Lara Downes, the New Yorker staff writer says music in America will keep evolving as long as the country keeps an open door to new people and new sounds.

(Image credit: Lara Downes)
item.dc:creator: Lara Downes
